news.headline: Paradigm Capital names Perimeter and maintains Profound as healthcare Top Picks
news.summary: Paradigm Capital names Perimeter a healthcare Top Pick with a Speculative Buy and a C$0.95 price target (price on July 3: C$0.275). The analyst sees the FDA approval of Claire as an accelerant for the rollout and points to M&A potential: the stock trades at 2.3x 2027e revenue, while comparable MedTech acquisitions paid 10.3x on average.

news.headline: Perimeter to Participate in the Planet MicroCap Showcase in Las Vegas
news.summary: CEO Adrian Mendes presented the commercialization strategy and upcoming milestones on June 17. It shows the focus on greater visibility with small-cap investors following the FDA approval.

news.headline: Perimeter Selected for INOVAIT Pilot Fund to Advance AI for Breast Cancer Surgery
news.summary: Perimeter receives up to ~C$148,000 from the Canadian INOVAIT Pilot Fund plus C$100,000 in Mitacs funding to develop the next AI generation for Claire with the University of Toronto. A small amount, but non-dilutive validation of the AI platform.

news.headline: Perimeter Closes Final Tranche of Non-Brokered Private Placement
news.summary: Final tranche of the convertible-note placement closed: C$6.04M in total. Notably, CEO Mendes subscribed C$2.76M and Chamath's SC Master Holdings (Social Capital) C$2.78M - strong insider alignment, but potential dilution.

news.headline: Perimeter Announces First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
news.summary: Q1: revenue ~$385k (recurring only, +14% YoY recurring), opex -30% to $3.2M, net loss improved to $3.0M. Critical: cash at end of March only ~$421k - before the ~$7.5M from the LIFE offering in May. First two commercial Claire placements, funnel at ~50 qualified leads.

news.headline: Perimeter Medical Imaging AI Closes LIFE Offering (~$7.5M)
news.summary: Closing of the LIFE offering with roughly $7.5M in gross proceeds - it secured survival given the nearly empty coffers and funds the U.S. launch of Claire. Together with the convertible notes, the balance sheet is stabilized in the short term, at the price of further dilution.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 numbers
catalysts.detail: Next quarterly report with the first full quarter of Claire commercialization; focus on new placements from the ~50-lead funnel and cash burn.

catalysts.label: Claire rollout / new clinic deals
catalysts.detail: Further commercial Claire placements at U.S. clinics expected - over a dozen leads in active negotiations.

catalysts.label: Medicare reimbursement decision
catalysts.detail: Per Paradigm Capital, expected around year-end - the most important value driver for adoption.

catalysts.label: Possible Nasdaq listing
catalysts.detail: Cited by Paradigm Capital as a catalyst to broaden investor access; no official timeline.

pulse: Following the FDA PMA approval of Claire (March 2026, the first AI-powered imaging device for breast-cancer margin assessment in the U.S.), Perimeter is transitioning from the development to the commercialization phase - the market is watching the pace of new clinic placements and the possible Medicare reimbursement at year-end. The funding situation was precarious (only $421k cash at end of Q1) but was secured through the ~$7.5M LIFE offering and C$6M in convertible notes, almost entirely subscribed by CEO Mendes and Chamath Palihapitiya's Social Capital - a strong insider signal. The price (~C$0.27 / US$0.20) is trading sideways; Paradigm sees clear upside with a C$0.95 target and M&A speculation, offset by dilution and the thin revenue base.
